panels.description: RDS Engine
panels.title: Engine

panels.description: RDS Engine version
panels.title: Engine Version

panels.description: Time since RDS instance creation
panels.title: Age

panels.description: RDS instance class (db.<family>.<instance size>)
panels.title: Instance class

panels.description: Total number of vCPU

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_vcpu_average{}) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(aws_account_id,aws_region,dbidentifier)
    max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (aws_account_id, aws_region, dbidentifier, instance_class)
)

panels.title: vCPU

panels.description: Total memory

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_memory_bytes{}) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(dbidentifier) max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (dbidentifier, instance_class)
)

panels.title: Memory

panels.description: Total disk storage
panels.title: Allocated storage

panels.description: Storage class type)
panels.title: Storage type

panels.title: Multi AZ

panels.title: Role

panels.title: Replicas

panels.description: Primary instance name for a replica)
panels.title: Source database

panels.description: AWS RDS instance internal reference
panels.title: DBI resource ID

panels.description: Certificate Authority bundle used by this RDS instance for TLS connections
panels.title: CA certificate identifier

panels.description: AWS RDS snapshot retention. This is not related to AWS Backup service
panels.title: Snapshot retention

panels.description: Current CPU usage
panels.title: CPU

panels.title: Memory

panels.title: Disk IOPS

panels.description: Current disk storage usage
panels.title: Storage

panels.description: Replication lag for replica RDS instance
panels.title: Replication lag

panels.description: Current RDS instance status
panels.title: Instance status

panels.description: Yes when RDS instance need a modification (e.g. Apply new parameter group settings). Modification could be scheduled during maintenance windows or triggered manually.
panels.title: Pending modification

panels.description: AWS may required hardward or software maintenance. Maintenance could be scheduled during maintenance windows or triggered manually.
panels.title: Pending maintenance

panels.description: Number of active queries waiting for CPU and non CPU wait. For optimal performances, you should not have more CPU wait that number of CPU

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_vcpu_average{}) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(aws_account_id,aws_region,dbidentifier)
    max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (aws_account_id, aws_region, dbidentifier, instance_class)
)

panels.title: Active queries vs vCPU

panels.description: Ratio of CPU usage
panels.title: CPU usage

panels.description: The number of client network connections to the database instance
panels.title: Database connections

panels.description: Total of read and write disk IOPS regarding instance IOPS limits. For optimal performances, you should not reach IOPS limits
panels.title: Disk IOPS usage

panels.description: The amount of available Random Access Memory

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_memory_bytes{}) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(dbidentifier) max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (dbidentifier, instance_class)
)

panels.title: Memory usage

panels.description: The average number of bytes read/write from disk per second. For optimal performances, you should not reach disk throughput
panels.title: Disk throughput

panels.description: Amount of swap space used on the DB instance. There's nothing wrong with a used SWAP, but for optimial perfomance you should avoid frequent changes
panels.title: Swap usage

panels.description: Storage size per type. WAL only applied to PostgreSQL
panels.title: Storage usage

panels.description: RDS instance status. Some AWS operations could not be performed when instance is not in 'available' status
panels.title: Status

panels.description: Ratio of free disk space
panels.title: Used storage

panels.description: The number of transactions currently used. Hard limit to 2 billion transactions (See Transaction ID Wraparound)
panels.title: Used Transactions

panels.description: Lag of PostgreSQL replica instances
panels.title: Replication lag

panels.description: Regardless of the allocated disk IOPS, the EC2 instance behind RDS also has disk IOPS limits. You can't use more IOPS than EC2's instance limit

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_max_iops_average{}) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(aws_account_id,aws_region,dbidentifier)
    max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (aws_account_id, aws_region, dbidentifier, instance_class)
) by (instance_class)

panels.title: Disk IOPS

panels.description: If RDS storage autoscaling is enabled, display the current usage
panels.title: RDS storage autoscaling usage

panels.description: Regardless of the allocated disk throughput, the EC2 instance behind RDS also has disk throughput limits. You can't use more throughput than EC2's instance limit

targets.expr:
max(
max(rds_instance_max_throughput_bytes{}) by (instance_class)
* on (instance_class)
group_left(aws_account_id,aws_region,dbidentifier)
max(rds_instance_info{aws_account_id="$aws_account_id",aws_region="$aws_region",dbidentifier="$dbidentifier"}) by (aws_account_id, aws_region, dbidentifier, instance_class)
) by (instance_class)

panels.title: Disk throughput
